core_mechanism: parameter-driven KAM-type breakup of invariant tori in a Hamiltonian dynamical system, producing a transition from integrable (regular) motion to a mixed regular-chaotic phase space, diagnosed via Poincaré sections, Lyapunov exponents, and Kolmogorov-Sinai entropy
brief_reason: The abstract tracks how increasing control parameters deform and fragment invariant tori and grow the Lyapunov exponents and KS entropy, i.e. the classic route by which a nearly-integrable Hamiltonian system develops coexisting regular and chaotic regions in phase space.
